ops.text: `cc-present push --session <name>` ignores the session value for subject resolution on a local daemon: three different --session values (including a unique timestamped one) all resolved to the same per-daemon subject (e288c370…), so two concurrent lanes pushing "separate" boards silently co-tenant one event log — a verification lane watched an interloper doc.replaced ("Tick E2E") land mid-run on the subject it had just seeded, and only exact-toast-text assertions kept the evidence clean. Meanwhile the contract documents GET /api/sessions as listing multiple open artifacts, so either --session should mint/route to a distinct subject or its no-op nature on the daemon should be documented.
